This is used only for reading or\n46:  * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},\n47:  * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n48:  */\n49: public class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n50:     public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n51: \n52:     private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n53: \n54:     private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n55: \n56:     private int validBytes = -1;\n57: \n58:     // Construct a writable memory records\n59:     private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n60:         Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n61:         this.buffer = buffer;\n62:     }\n63: \n64:     @Override\n65:     public int sizeInBytes() {\n66:         return buffer.limit();\n67:     }\n68: \n69:     @Override\n70:     public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n71:         if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n72:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n73:                     + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n74: \n75:         return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n76:     }\n77: \n78:     /**\n79:      * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n80:      * @param channel The channel to write to\n81:      * @return The number of bytes written\n82:      * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n83:      */\n84:     public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n85:         buffer.mark();\n86:         int written = 0;\n87:         while (written < sizeInBytes())\n88:             written += channel.write(buffer);\n89:         buffer.reset();\n90:         return written;\n91:     }\n92: \n93:     /**\n94:      * The total number of bytes in this message set not including any partial, trailing messages. This\n95:      * may be smaller than what is returned by {@link #sizeInBytes()}.\n96:      * @return The number of valid bytes\n97:      */\n98:     public int validBytes() {\n99:         if (validBytes >= 0)\n100:             return validBytes;\n101: \n102:         int bytes = 0;\n103:         for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n104:             bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n105: \n106:         this.validBytes = bytes;\n107:         return bytes;\n108:     }\n109: \n110:     @Override\n111:     public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n112:         return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n113:     }\n114: \n115:     /**\n116:      * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\n117:      * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\n118:      *         magic byte, null otherwise\n119:      * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\n120:      */\n121:     public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n122:         if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n123:             return null;\n124:         return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n125:     }\n126: \n127:     /**\n128:      * Filter the records into the provided ByteBuffer.\n129:      *\n130:      * @param filter                      The filter function\n131:      * @param destinationBuffer           The byte buffer to write the filtered records to\n132:      * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported. For small\n133:      *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4) will\n134:      *                                    dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n135:      *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n136:      *                                    performance impact.\n137:      * @return A FilterResult with a summary of the output (for metrics) and potentially an overflow buffer\n138:      */\n139:     public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n140:         return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n141:     }\n142: \n143:     /**\n144:      * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n145:      * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n146:      */\n147:     private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n148:                                          ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n149:         FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n150:         ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n151:         for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n152:             final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n153:             final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n154:             final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n155: \n156:             filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n157: \n158:             if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n159:                 continue;\n160: \n161:             final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n162:                 filter);\n163:             List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n164:             boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n165:             boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n166:             long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n167: \n168:             if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n169:                 // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n170:                 // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n171:                 // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n172:                 boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n173:                     batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n174:                 if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n175:                     batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n176:                     filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n177:                 } else {\n178:                     long deleteHorizonMs;\n179:                     if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n180:                         deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n181:                     else\n182:                         deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n183:                     try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n184:                             bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n185:                         MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n186:                         int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n187:                         MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n188:                         filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n189:                             maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n190:                     }\n191:                 }\n192:             } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n193:                 if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n194:                     throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n195: \n196:                 bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n197:                 DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n198:                         batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n199:                         batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n200:                         batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n201: ",

}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"200:                         batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\\n201:                 filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\\n202:             }\\n203: \\n204:             // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\\n205:             // avoid the need for additional allocations.\\n206:             ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\\n207:             if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\\n208:                 filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\\n209:                 return filterResult;\\n210:             }\\n211:         }\\n212: \\n213:         return filterResult;\\n214:     }\\n215: \\n216:     private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\\n217:                                                  BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\\n218:                                                  FilterResult filterResult,\\n219:                                                  RecordFilter filter) {\\n220:         try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\\n221:             long maxOffset = -1;\\n222:             boolean containsTombstones = false;\\n223:             // Convert records with old record versions\\n224:             boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\\n225:             List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\\n226:             while (iterator.hasNext()) {\\n227:                 Record record = iterator.next();\\n228:                 filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\\n229: \\n230:                 if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\\n231:                     if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\\n232:                         maxOffset = record.offset();\\n233: \\n234:                     retainedRecords.add(record);\\n235: \\n236:                     if (!record.hasValue()) {\\n237:                         containsTombstones = true;\\n238:                     }\\n239:                 } else {\\n240:                     writeOriginalBatch = false;\\n241:                 }\\n242:             }\\n243:             return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\\n244:         }\\n245:     }\\n246: \\n247:     private static class BatchFilterResult {\\n248:         private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\\n249:         private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\\n250:         private final boolean containsTombstones;\\n251:         private final long maxOffset;\\n252:         private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\\n253:                                   final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\\n254:                                   final boolean containsTombstones,\\n255:                                   final long maxOffset) {\\n256:             this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\\n257:             this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\\n258:             this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\\n259:             this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\\n260:         }\\n261:     }\\n262: \\n263:     private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\\n264:                                                                  List<Record> retainedRecords,\\n265:                                                                  ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\\n266:                                                                  final long deleteHorizonMs) {\\n267:         Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\\n268:         // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\\n269:         // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\\n270:         // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\\n271:         // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\\n272:         TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\\n273:                 TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\\n274:         long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\\n275:                 originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\\n276:         long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\\n277:                 originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\\n278: \\n279:         // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\\n280:         MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\\n281:                 compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\\n282:                 originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\\n283:                 originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\\n284: \\n285:         for (Record record : retainedRecords)\\n286:             builder.append(record);\\n287: \\n288:         if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\\n289:             // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\\n290:             // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\\n291:             // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\\n292:             builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\\n293: \\n294:         return builder;\\n295:     }\\n296: \\n297:     /**\\n298:      * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\\n299:      */\\n300:     public ByteBuffer buffer() {\\n301:         return buffer.duplicate();\\n302:     }\\n303: \\n304:     @Override\\n305:     public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\\n306:         if (position < 0)\\n307:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Invalid position: \\\" + position + \\\" in read from \\\" + this);\\n308:         if (position > buffer.limit())\\n309:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Slice from position \\\" + position + \\\" exceeds end position of \\\" + this);\\n310:         if (size < 0)\\n311:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Invalid size: \\\" + size + \\\" in read from \\\" + this);\\n312: \\n313:         int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\\n314:         // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\\n315:         // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\\n316:         // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\\n317:         // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\\n318:         // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\\n319:         // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\\n320:         ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\\n321:         slicedBuffer.position(position);\\n322:         slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\\n323:         // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\\n324:         slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\\n325: \\n326:         return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\\n327:     }\\n328: \\n329:     @Override\\n330:     public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\\n331:         return batches;\\n332:     }\\n333: \\n334:     @Override\\n335:     public String toString() {\\n336:         return \\\"MemoryRecords(size=\\\" + sizeInBytes() +\\n337:                 \\\", buffer=\\\" + buffer +\\n338:                 \\\")\\\";\\n339:     }\\n340: \\n341:     @Override\\n342:     public boolean equals(Object o) {\\n343:         if (this == o)\\n344:             return true;\\n345:         if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\\n346:             return false;\\n347: \\n348:         MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\\n349: \\n350:         return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\\n351:     }\\n352: \\n353:     @Override\\n354:     public int hashCode() {\\n355:         return buffer.hashCode();\\n356:     }\\n357: \\n358:     public abstract static class RecordFilter {\\n359:         public final long currentTime;\\n360:         public final long deleteRetentionMs;\\n361: \\n362:         public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\\n363:             this.currentTime = currentTime;\\n364:             this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\\n365:         }\\n366: \\n367:         public static class BatchRetentionResult {\\n368:             public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\\n369:             public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\\n370:             public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\\n371:                                         final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\\n372:                 this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\\n373:                 this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\\n374:             }\\n375:         }\\n376: \\n377:         public enum BatchRetention {\\n378:             DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\\n379:             RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\\n380:             DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\\n381:         }\\n382: \\n383:         /**\\n384:          * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\\n385:          * check the records individually).\\n386:          */\\n387:         protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\\n388: \\n389:         /**\\n390:          * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\\n391:          * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\\n392:          * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\\n393:          */\\n394:         protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\\n395:     }\\n396: \\n397:     public static class FilterResult {\\n398:         private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\\n399:         private int messagesRead = 0;\\n400:         // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\\n401: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java%3FL200-400\"}",

    "content": "200:                         batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n201:                 filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n202:             }\n203: \n204:             // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n205:             // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n206:             ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n207:             if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n208:                 filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n209:                 return filterResult;\n210:             }\n211:         }\n212: \n213:         return filterResult;\n214:     }\n215: \n216:     private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n217:                                                  BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n218:                                                  FilterResult filterResult,\n219:                                                  RecordFilter filter) {\n220:         try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n221:             long maxOffset = -1;\n222:             boolean containsTombstones = false;\n223:             // Convert records with old record versions\n224:             boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n225:             List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n226:             while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n227:                 Record record = iterator.next();\n228:                 filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n229: \n230:                 if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n231:                     if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n232:                         maxOffset = record.offset();\n233: \n234:                     retainedRecords.add(record);\n235: \n236:                     if (!record.hasValue()) {\n237:                         containsTombstones = true;\n238:                     }\n239:                 } else {\n240:                     writeOriginalBatch = false;\n241:                 }\n242:             }\n243:             return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n244:         }\n245:     }\n246: \n247:     private static class BatchFilterResult {\n248:         private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n249:         private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n250:         private final boolean containsTombstones;\n251:         private final long maxOffset;\n252:         private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n253:                                   final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n254:                                   final boolean containsTombstones,\n255:                                   final long maxOffset) {\n256:             this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n257:             this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n258:             this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n259:             this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n260:         }\n261:     }\n262: \n263:     private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n264:                                                                  List<Record> retainedRecords,\n265:                                                                  ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n266:                                                                  final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n267:         Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n268:         // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n269:         // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n270:         // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n271:         // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n272:         TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n273:                 TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n274:         long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n275:                 originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n276:         long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n277:                 originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n278: \n279:         // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n280:         MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n281:                 compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n282:                 originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n283:                 originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n284: \n285:         for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n286:             builder.append(record);\n287: \n288:         if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n289:             // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n290:             // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n291:             // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n292:             builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n293: \n294:         return builder;\n295:     }\n296: \n297:     /**\n298:      * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n299:      */\n300:     public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n301:         return buffer.duplicate();\n302:     }\n303: \n304:     @Override\n305:     public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n306:         if (position < 0)\n307:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n308:         if (position > buffer.limit())\n309:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n310:         if (size < 0)\n311:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n312: \n313:         int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n314:         // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n315:         // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n316:         // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n317:         // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n318:         // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n319:         // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n320:         ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n321:         slicedBuffer.position(position);\n322:         slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n323:         // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n324:         slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n325: \n326:         return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n327:     }\n328: \n329:     @Override\n330:     public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n331:         return batches;\n332:     }\n333: \n334:     @Override\n335:     public String toString() {\n336:         return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n337:                 \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n338:                 \")\";\n339:     }\n340: \n341:     @Override\n342:     public boolean equals(Object o) {\n343:         if (this == o)\n344:             return true;\n345:         if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n346:             return false;\n347: \n348:         MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n349: \n350:         return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n351:     }\n352: \n353:     @Override\n354:     public int hashCode() {\n355:         return buffer.hashCode();\n356:     }\n357: \n358:     public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n359:         public final long currentTime;\n360:         public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n361: \n362:         public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n363:             this.currentTime = currentTime;\n364:             this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n365:         }\n366: \n367:         public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n368:             public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n369:             public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n370:             public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n371:                                         final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n372:                 this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n373:                 this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n374:             }\n375:         }\n376: \n377:         public enum BatchRetention {\n378:             DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n379:             RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n380:             DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n381:         }\n382: \n383:         /**\n384:          * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n385:          * check the records individually).\n386:          */\n387:         protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n388: \n389:         /**\n390:          * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n391:          * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n392:          * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n393:          */\n394:         protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n395:     }\n396: \n397:     public static class FilterResult {\n398:         private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n399:         private int messagesRead = 0;\n400:         // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n401: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\\n6:  * (the \\\"License\\\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8:  *\\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10:  *\\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15:  * limitations under the License.\\n16:  */\\n17: package org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\\n18: \\n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.InvalidRecordException;\\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\\n24: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteUtils;\\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Crc32C;\\n30: \\n31: import java.io.IOException;\\n32: import java.io.InputStream;\\n33: import java.nio.BufferUnderflowException;\\n34: import java.nio.ByteBuffer;\\n35: import java.util.ArrayList;\\n36: import java.util.Collections;\\n37: import java.util.Iterator;\\n38: import java.util.List;\\n39: import java.util.NoSuchElementException;\\n40: import java.util.Objects;\\n41: import java.util.OptionalLong;\\n42: \\n43: import static org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.Records.LOG_OVERHEAD;\\n44: \\n45: /**\\n46:  * RecordBatch implementation for magic 2 and above. The schema is given below:\\n47:  *\\n48:  * RecordBatch =>\\n49:  *  BaseOffset => Int64\\n50:  *  Length => Int32\\n51:  *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\\n52:  *  Magic => Int8\\n53:  *  CRC => Uint32\\n54:  *  Attributes => Int16\\n55:  *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta\\n56:  *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\\n57:  *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\\n58:  *  ProducerId => Int64\\n59:  *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\\n60:  *  BaseSequence => Int32\\n61:  *  RecordsCount => Int32\\n62:  *  Records => [Record]\\n63:  *\\n64:  * Note that when compression is enabled (see attributes below), the compressed record data is serialized\\n65:  * directly following the count of the number of records.\\n66:  *\\n67:  * The CRC covers the data from the attributes to the end of the batch (i.e. all the bytes that follow the CRC). It is\\n68:  * located after the magic byte, which means that clients must parse the magic byte before deciding how to interpret\\n69:  * the bytes between the batch length and the magic byte. The partition leader epoch field is not included in the CRC\\n70:  * computation to avoid the need to recompute the CRC when this field is assigned for every batch that is received by\\n71:  * the broker. The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used for the computation.\\n72:  *\\n73:  * On Compaction: Unlike the older message formats, magic v2 and above preserves the first and last offset/sequence\\n74:  * numbers from the original batch when the log is cleaned. This is required in order to be able to restore the\\n75:  * producer's state when the log is reloaded. If we did not retain the last sequence number, then following\\n76:  * a partition leader failure, once the new leader has rebuilt the producer state from the log, the next sequence\\n77:  * expected number would no longer be in sync with what was written by the client. This would cause an\\n78:  * unexpected OutOfOrderSequence error, which is typically fatal. The base sequence number must be preserved for\\n79:  * duplicate checking: the broker checks incoming Produce requests for duplicates by verifying that the first and\\n80:  * last sequence numbers of the incoming batch match the last from that producer.\\n81:  *\\n82:  * Note that if all of the records in a batch are removed during compaction, the broker may still retain an empty\\n83:  * batch header in order to preserve the producer sequence information as described above. These empty batches\\n84:  * are retained only until either a new sequence number is written by the corresponding producer or the producerId\\n85:  * is expired from lack of activity.\\n86:  *\\n87:  * There is no similar need to preserve the timestamp from the original batch after compaction. The BaseTimestamp\\n88:  * field therefore reflects the timestamp of the first record in the batch in most cases. If the batch is empty, the\\n89:  * BaseTimestamp will be set to -1 (NO_TIMESTAMP). If the delete horizon flag is set to 1, the BaseTimestamp\\n90:  * will be set to the time at which tombstone records and aborted transaction markers in the batch should be removed.\\n91:  *\\n92:  * Similarly, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the maximum timestamp of the current records if the timestamp type\\n93:  * is CREATE_TIME. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, on the other hand, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the timestamp set\\n94:  * by the broker and is preserved after compaction. Additionally, the MaxTimestamp of an empty batch always retains\\n95:  * the previous value prior to becoming empty.\\n96:  *\\n97:  * The current attributes are given below:\\n98:  *\\n99:  *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n100:  *  | Unused (7-15) | Delete Horizon Flag (6) | Control (5) | Transactional (4) | Timestamp Type (3) | Compression Type (0-2) |\\n101:  *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n102:  */\\n103: public class DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch {\\n104:     static final int BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET = 0;\\n105:     static final int BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH = 8;\\n106:     static final int LENGTH_OFFSET = BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET + BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH;\\n107:     static final int LENGTH_LENGTH = 4;\\n108:     static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET = LENGTH_OFFSET + LENGTH_LENGTH;\\n109:     static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 4;\\n110:     static final int MAGIC_OFFSET = PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\\n111:     static final int MAGIC_LENGTH = 1;\\n112:     public static final int CRC_OFFSET = MAGIC_OFFSET + MAGIC_LENGTH;\\n113:     static final int CRC_LENGTH = 4;\\n114:     static final int ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET = CRC_OFFSET + CRC_LENGTH;\\n115:     static final int ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH = 2;\\n116:     public static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET = ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET + ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH;\\n117:     static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH = 4;\\n118:     static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH;\\n119:     static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\\n120:     static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\\n121:     static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\\n122:     static final int PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET = MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\\n123:     static final int PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH = 8;\\n124:     static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET = PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET + PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH;\\n125:     static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 2;\\n126:     static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET = PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\\n127:     static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 4;\\n128:     public static final int RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET = BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET + BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH;\\n129:     static final int RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH = 4;\\n130:     static final int RECORDS_OFFSET = RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET + RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH;\\n131:     public static final int RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = RECORDS_OFFSET;\\n132: \\n133:     private static final byte COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK = 0x07;\\n134:     private static final byte TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK = 0x10;\\n135:     private static final int CONTROL_FLAG_MASK = 0x20;\\n136:     private static final byte DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK = 0x40;\\n137:     private static final byte TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK = 0x08;\\n138: \\n139:     private final ByteBuffer buffer;\\n140: \\n141:     DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\\n142:         this.buffer = buffer;\\n143:     }\\n144: \\n145:     @Override\\n146:     public byte magic() {\\n147:         return buffer.get(MAGIC_OFFSET);\\n148:     }\\n149: \\n150:     @Override\\n151:     public void ensureValid() {\\n152:         if (sizeInBytes() < RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\\n153:             throw new CorruptRecordException(\\\"Record batch is corrupt (the size \\\" + sizeInBytes() +\\n154:                     \\\" is smaller than the minimum allowed overhead \\\" + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + \\\")\\\");\\n155: \\n156:         if (!isValid())\\n157:             throw new CorruptRecordException(\\\"Record is corrupt (stored crc = \\\" + checksum()\\n158:                     + \\\", computed crc = \\\" + computeChecksum() + \\\")\\\");\\n159:     }\\n160: \\n161:     /**\\n162:      * Gets the base timestamp of the batch which is used to calculate the record timestamps from the deltas.\\n163:      *\\n164:      * @return The base timestamp\\n165:      */\\n166:     public long baseTimestamp() {\\n167:         return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\\n168:     }\\n169: \\n170:     @Override\\n171:     public long maxTimestamp() {\\n172:         return buffer.getLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\\n173:     }\\n174: \\n175:     @Override\\n176:     public TimestampType timestampType() {\\n177:         return (attributes() & TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK) == 0 ? TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME;\\n178:     }\\n179: \\n180:     @Override\\n181:     public long baseOffset() {\\n182:         return buffer.getLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET);\\n183:     }\\n184: \\n185:     @Override\\n186:     public long lastOffset() {\\n187:         return baseOffset() + lastOffsetDelta();\\n188:     }\\n189: \\n190:     @Override\\n191:     public long producerId() {\\n192:         return buffer.getLong(PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET);\\n193:     }\\n194: \\n195:     @Override\\n196:     public short producerEpoch() {\\n197:         return buffer.getShort(PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\\n198:     }\\n199: \\n200:     @Override\\n201:     public int baseSequence() {\\n202:         return buffer.getInt(BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET);\\n203:     }\\n204: \\n205:     private int lastOffsetDelta() {\\n206:         return buffer.getInt(LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET);\\n207:     }\\n208: \\n209:     @Override\\n210:     public int lastSequence() {\\n211:         int baseSequence = baseSequence();\\n212:         if (baseSequence == RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE)\\n213:             return RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE;\\n214:         return incrementSequence(baseSequence, lastOffsetDelta());\\n215:     }\\n216: \\n217:     @Override\\n218:     public CompressionType compressionType() {\\n219:         return CompressionType.forId(attributes() & COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK);\\n220:     }\\n221: \\n222:     @Override\\n223:     public int sizeInBytes() {\\n224:         return LOG_OVERHEAD + buffer.getInt(LENGTH_OFFSET);\\n225:     }\\n226: \\n227:     private int count() {\\n228:         return buffer.getInt(RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET);\\n229:     }\\n230: \\n231:     @Override\\n232:     public Integer countOrNull() {\\n233:         return count();\\n234:     }\\n235: \\n236:     @Override\\n237:     public void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer) {\\n238:         buffer.put(this.buffer.duplicate());\\n239:     }\\n240: \\n241:     @Override\\n242:     public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {\\n243:         outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());\\n244:     }\\n245: \\n246:     @Override\\n247:     public boolean isTransactional() {\\n248:         return (attributes() & TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\\n249:     }\\n250: \\n251:     private boolean hasDeleteHorizonMs() {\\n252:         return (attributes() & DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\\n253:     }\\n254: \\n255:     @Override\\n256:     public OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs() {\\n257:         if (hasDeleteHorizonMs())\\n258:             return OptionalLong.of(buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET));\\n259:         else\\n260:             return OptionalLong.empty();\\n261:     }\\n262: \\n263:     @Override\\n264:     public boolean isControlBatch() {\\n265:         return (attributes() & CONTROL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\\n266:     }\\n267: \\n268:     @Override\\n269:     public int partitionLeaderEpoch() {\\n270:         return buffer.getInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\\n271:     }\\n272: \\n273:     public InputStream recordInputStream(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\\n274:         final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\\n275:         buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\\n276:         return Compression.of(compressionType()).build().wrapForInput(buffer, magic(), bufferSupplier);\\n277:     }\\n278: \\n279:     private CloseableIterator<Record> compressedIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier, boolean skipKeyValue) {\\n280:         final InputStream inputStream = recordInputStream(bufferSupplier);\\n281: \\n282:         if (skipKeyValue) {\\n283:             return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\\n284:                 @Override\\n285:                 protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\\n286:                     return DefaultRecord.readPartiallyFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\\n287:                 }\\n288:             };\\n289:         } else {\\n290:             return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\\n291:                 @Override\\n292:                 protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\\n293:                     return DefaultRecord.readFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\\n294:                 }\\n295:             };\\n296:         }\\n297:     }\\n298: \\n299:     private CloseableIterator<Record> uncompressedIterator() {\\n300:         final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\\n301:         buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\\n302:         return new RecordIterator() {\\n303:             @Override\\n304:             protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\\n305:                 try {\\n306:                     return DefaultRecord.readFrom(buffer, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\\n307:                 } catch (BufferUnderflowException e) {\\n308:                     throw new InvalidRecordException(\\\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\\\");\\n309:                 }\\n310:             }\\n311:             @Override\\n312:             protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\\n313:                 return !buffer.hasRemaining();\\n314:             }\\n315:             @Override\\n316:             public void close() {}\\n317:         };\\n318:     }\\n319: \\n320:     @Override\\n321:     public Iterator<Record> iterator() {\\n322:         if (count() == 0)\\n323:             return Collections.emptyIterator();\\n324: \\n325:         if (!isCompressed())\\n326:             return uncompressedIterator();\\n327: \\n328:         // for a normal iterator, we cannot ensure that the underlying compression stream is closed,\\n329:         // so we decompress the full record set here. Use cases which call for a lower memory footprint\\n330:         // can use `streamingIterator` at the cost of additional complexity\\n331:         try (CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = compressedIterator(BufferSupplier.NO_CACHING, false)) {\\n332:             List<Record> records = new ArrayList<>(count());\\n333:             while (iterator.hasNext())\\n334:                 records.add(iterator.next());\\n335:             return records.iterator();\\n336:         }\\n337:     }\\n338: \\n339:     @Override\\n340:     public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\\n341:         if (count() == 0) {\\n342:             return CloseableIterator.wrap(Collections.emptyIterator());\\n343:         }\\n344: \\n345:         /*\\n346:          * For uncompressed iterator, it is actually not worth skipping key / value / headers at all since\\n347:          * its ByteBufferInputStream's skip() function is less efficient compared with just reading it actually\\n348:          * as it will allocate new byte array.\\n349:          */\\n350:         if (!isCompressed())\\n351:             return uncompressedIterator();\\n352: \\n353:         // we define this to be a closable iterator so that caller (i.e. the log validator) needs to close it\\n354:         // while we can save memory footprint of not decompressing the full record set ahead of time\\n355:         return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, true);\\n356:     }\\n357: \\n358:     @Override\\n359:     public CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\\n360:         if (isCompressed())\\n361:             return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, false);\\n362:         else\\n363:             return uncompressedIterator();\\n364:     }\\n365: \\n366:     @Override\\n367:     public void setLastOffset(long offset) {\\n368:         buffer.putLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, offset - lastOffsetDelta());\\n369:     }\\n370: \\n371:     @Override\\n372:     public void setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType timestampType, long maxTimestamp) {\\n373:         long currentMaxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\\n374:         // We don't need to recompute crc if the timestamp is not updated.\\n375:         if (timestampType() == timestampType && currentMaxTimestamp == maxTimestamp)\\n376:             return;\\n377: \\n378:         byte attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType(), timestampType, isTransactional(), isControlBatch(), hasDeleteHorizonMs());\\n379:         buffer.putShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\\n380:         buffer.putLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\\n381:         long crc = computeChecksum();\\n382:         ByteUtils.writeUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET, crc);\\n383:     }\\n384: \\n385:     @Override\\n386:     public void setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int epoch) {\\n387:         buffer.putInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\\n388:     }\\n389: \\n390:     @Override\\n391:     public long checksum() {\\n392:         return ByteUtils.readUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET);\\n393:     }\\n394: \\n395:     public boolean isValid() {\\n396:         return sizeInBytes() >= RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD && checksum() == computeChecksum();\\n397:     }\\n398: \\n399:     private long computeChecksum() {\\n400:         return Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, buffer.limit() - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\\n401:     }\\n402: \\n403:     private byte attributes() {\\n404:         // note we're not using the second byte of attributes\\n405:         return (byte) buffer.getShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\\n406:     }\\n407: \\n408:     @Override\\n409:     public boolean equals(Object o) {\\n410:         if (this == o)\\n411:             return true;\\n412:         if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\\n413:             return false;\\n414: \\n415:         DefaultRecordBatch that = (DefaultRecordBatch) o;\\n416:         return Objects.equals(buffer, that.buffer);\\n417:     }\\n418: \\n419:     @Override\\n420:     public int hashCode() {\\n421:         return buffer != null ? buffer.hashCode() : 0;\\n422:     }\\n423: \\n424:     private static byte computeAttributes(CompressionType type, TimestampType timestampType,\\n425:                                           boolean isTransactional, boolean isControl, boolean isDeleteHorizonSet) {\\n426:         if (timestampType == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE)\\n427:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\\\"Timestamp type must be provided to compute attributes for message \\\" +\\n428:                     \\\"format v2 and above\\\");\\n429: \\n430:         byte attributes = isTransactional ? TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK : 0;\\n431:         if (isControl)\\n432:             attributes |= CONTROL_FLAG_MASK;\\n433:         if (type.id > 0)\\n434:             attributes |= (byte) (COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK & type.id);\\n435:         if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\\n436:             attributes |= TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK;\\n437:         if (isDeleteHorizonSet)\\n438:             attributes |= DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK;\\n439:         return attributes;\\n440:     }\\n441: \\n442:     public static void writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\\n443:                                         byte magic,\\n444:                                         long producerId,\\n445:                                         short producerEpoch,\\n446:                                         int baseSequence,\\n447:                                         long baseOffset,\\n448:                                         long lastOffset,\\n449:                                         int partitionLeaderEpoch,\\n450:                                         TimestampType timestampType,\\n451: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java%3FL1-450\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2:  * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n3:  * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n4:  * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n5:  * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n6:  * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n7:  * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8:  *\n9:  *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10:  *\n11:  * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12:  * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13:  * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14:  * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15:  * limitations under the License.\n16:  */\n17: package org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n18: \n19: import org.apache.kafka.common.InvalidRecordException;\n20: import org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaException;\n21: import org.apache.kafka.common.compress.Compression;\n22: import org.apache.kafka.common.errors.CorruptRecordException;\n23: import org.apache.kafka.common.header.Header;\n24: import org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\n25: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\n26: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteBufferOutputStream;\n27: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteUtils;\n28: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n29: import org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Crc32C;\n30: \n31: import java.io.IOException;\n32: import java.io.InputStream;\n33: import java.nio.BufferUnderflowException;\n34: import java.nio.ByteBuffer;\n35: import java.util.ArrayList;\n36: import java.util.Collections;\n37: import java.util.Iterator;\n38: import java.util.List;\n39: import java.util.NoSuchElementException;\n40: import java.util.Objects;\n41: import java.util.OptionalLong;\n42: \n43: import static org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.Records.LOG_OVERHEAD;\n44: \n45: /**\n46:  * RecordBatch implementation for magic 2 and above. The schema is given below:\n47:  *\n48:  * RecordBatch =>\n49:  *  BaseOffset => Int64\n50:  *  Length => Int32\n51:  *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\n52:  *  Magic => Int8\n53:  *  CRC => Uint32\n54:  *  Attributes => Int16\n55:  *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta\n56:  *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\n57:  *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\n58:  *  ProducerId => Int64\n59:  *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\n60:  *  BaseSequence => Int32\n61:  *  RecordsCount => Int32\n62:  *  Records => [Record]\n63:  *\n64:  * Note that when compression is enabled (see attributes below), the compressed record data is serialized\n65:  * directly following the count of the number of records.\n66:  *\n67:  * The CRC covers the data from the attributes to the end of the batch (i.e. all the bytes that follow the CRC). It is\n68:  * located after the magic byte, which means that clients must parse the magic byte before deciding how to interpret\n69:  * the bytes between the batch length and the magic byte. The partition leader epoch field is not included in the CRC\n70:  * computation to avoid the need to recompute the CRC when this field is assigned for every batch that is received by\n71:  * the broker. The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial is used for the computation.\n72:  *\n73:  * On Compaction: Unlike the older message formats, magic v2 and above preserves the first and last offset/sequence\n74:  * numbers from the original batch when the log is cleaned. This is required in order to be able to restore the\n75:  * producer's state when the log is reloaded. If we did not retain the last sequence number, then following\n76:  * a partition leader failure, once the new leader has rebuilt the producer state from the log, the next sequence\n77:  * expected number would no longer be in sync with what was written by the client. This would cause an\n78:  * unexpected OutOfOrderSequence error, which is typically fatal. The base sequence number must be preserved for\n79:  * duplicate checking: the broker checks incoming Produce requests for duplicates by verifying that the first and\n80:  * last sequence numbers of the incoming batch match the last from that producer.\n81:  *\n82:  * Note that if all of the records in a batch are removed during compaction, the broker may still retain an empty\n83:  * batch header in order to preserve the producer sequence information as described above. These empty batches\n84:  * are retained only until either a new sequence number is written by the corresponding producer or the producerId\n85:  * is expired from lack of activity.\n86:  *\n87:  * There is no similar need to preserve the timestamp from the original batch after compaction. The BaseTimestamp\n88:  * field therefore reflects the timestamp of the first record in the batch in most cases. If the batch is empty, the\n89:  * BaseTimestamp will be set to -1 (NO_TIMESTAMP). If the delete horizon flag is set to 1, the BaseTimestamp\n90:  * will be set to the time at which tombstone records and aborted transaction markers in the batch should be removed.\n91:  *\n92:  * Similarly, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the maximum timestamp of the current records if the timestamp type\n93:  * is CREATE_TIME. For LOG_APPEND_TIME, on the other hand, the MaxTimestamp field reflects the timestamp set\n94:  * by the broker and is preserved after compaction. Additionally, the MaxTimestamp of an empty batch always retains\n95:  * the previous value prior to becoming empty.\n96:  *\n97:  * The current attributes are given below:\n98:  *\n99:  *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n100:  *  | Unused (7-15) | Delete Horizon Flag (6) | Control (5) | Transactional (4) | Timestamp Type (3) | Compression Type (0-2) |\n101:  *  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------\n102:  */\n103: public class DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch {\n104:     static final int BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET = 0;\n105:     static final int BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH = 8;\n106:     static final int LENGTH_OFFSET = BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET + BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH;\n107:     static final int LENGTH_LENGTH = 4;\n108:     static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET = LENGTH_OFFSET + LENGTH_LENGTH;\n109:     static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 4;\n110:     static final int MAGIC_OFFSET = PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n111:     static final int MAGIC_LENGTH = 1;\n112:     public static final int CRC_OFFSET = MAGIC_OFFSET + MAGIC_LENGTH;\n113:     static final int CRC_LENGTH = 4;\n114:     static final int ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET = CRC_OFFSET + CRC_LENGTH;\n115:     static final int ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH = 2;\n116:     public static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET = ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET + ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH;\n117:     static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH = 4;\n118:     static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH;\n119:     static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n120:     static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n121:     static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n122:     static final int PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET = MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n123:     static final int PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH = 8;\n124:     static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET = PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET + PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH;\n125:     static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 2;\n126:     static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET = PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n127:     static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 4;\n128:     public static final int RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET = BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET + BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH;\n129:     static final int RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH = 4;\n130:     static final int RECORDS_OFFSET = RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET + RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH;\n131:     public static final int RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = RECORDS_OFFSET;\n132: \n133:     private static final byte COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK = 0x07;\n134:     private static final byte TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK = 0x10;\n135:     private static final int CONTROL_FLAG_MASK = 0x20;\n136:     private static final byte DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK = 0x40;\n137:     private static final byte TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK = 0x08;\n138: \n139:     private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n140: \n141:     DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n142:         this.buffer = buffer;\n143:     }\n144: \n145:     @Override\n146:     public byte magic() {\n147:         return buffer.get(MAGIC_OFFSET);\n148:     }\n149: \n150:     @Override\n151:     public void ensureValid() {\n152:         if (sizeInBytes() < RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\n153:             throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record batch is corrupt (the size \" + sizeInBytes() +\n154:                     \" is smaller than the minimum allowed overhead \" + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + \")\");\n155: \n156:         if (!isValid())\n157:             throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record is corrupt (stored crc = \" + checksum()\n158:                     + \", computed crc = \" + computeChecksum() + \")\");\n159:     }\n160: \n161:     /**\n162:      * Gets the base timestamp of the batch which is used to calculate the record timestamps from the deltas.\n163:      *\n164:      * @return The base timestamp\n165:      */\n166:     public long baseTimestamp() {\n167:         return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n168:     }\n169: \n170:     @Override\n171:     public long maxTimestamp() {\n172:         return buffer.getLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n173:     }\n174: \n175:     @Override\n176:     public TimestampType timestampType() {\n177:         return (attributes() & TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK) == 0 ? TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME;\n178:     }\n179: \n180:     @Override\n181:     public long baseOffset() {\n182:         return buffer.getLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET);\n183:     }\n184: \n185:     @Override\n186:     public long lastOffset() {\n187:         return baseOffset() + lastOffsetDelta();\n188:     }\n189: \n190:     @Override\n191:     public long producerId() {\n192:         return buffer.getLong(PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET);\n193:     }\n194: \n195:     @Override\n196:     public short producerEpoch() {\n197:         return buffer.getShort(PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n198:     }\n199: \n200:     @Override\n201:     public int baseSequence() {\n202:         return buffer.getInt(BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET);\n203:     }\n204: \n205:     private int lastOffsetDelta() {\n206:         return buffer.getInt(LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET);\n207:     }\n208: \n209:     @Override\n210:     public int lastSequence() {\n211:         int baseSequence = baseSequence();\n212:         if (baseSequence == RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE)\n213:             return RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE;\n214:         return incrementSequence(baseSequence, lastOffsetDelta());\n215:     }\n216: \n217:     @Override\n218:     public CompressionType compressionType() {\n219:         return CompressionType.forId(attributes() & COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK);\n220:     }\n221: \n222:     @Override\n223:     public int sizeInBytes() {\n224:         return LOG_OVERHEAD + buffer.getInt(LENGTH_OFFSET);\n225:     }\n226: \n227:     private int count() {\n228:         return buffer.getInt(RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET);\n229:     }\n230: \n231:     @Override\n232:     public Integer countOrNull() {\n233:         return count();\n234:     }\n235: \n236:     @Override\n237:     public void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n238:         buffer.put(this.buffer.duplicate());\n239:     }\n240: \n241:     @Override\n242:     public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {\n243:         outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());\n244:     }\n245: \n246:     @Override\n247:     public boolean isTransactional() {\n248:         return (attributes() & TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n249:     }\n250: \n251:     private boolean hasDeleteHorizonMs() {\n252:         return (attributes() & DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n253:     }\n254: \n255:     @Override\n256:     public OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs() {\n257:         if (hasDeleteHorizonMs())\n258:             return OptionalLong.of(buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET));\n259:         else\n260:             return OptionalLong.empty();\n261:     }\n262: \n263:     @Override\n264:     public boolean isControlBatch() {\n265:         return (attributes() & CONTROL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n266:     }\n267: \n268:     @Override\n269:     public int partitionLeaderEpoch() {\n270:         return buffer.getInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n271:     }\n272: \n273:     public InputStream recordInputStream(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n274:         final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n275:         buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n276:         return Compression.of(compressionType()).build().wrapForInput(buffer, magic(), bufferSupplier);\n277:     }\n278: \n279:     private CloseableIterator<Record> compressedIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier, boolean skipKeyValue) {\n280:         final InputStream inputStream = recordInputStream(bufferSupplier);\n281: \n282:         if (skipKeyValue) {\n283:             return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n284:                 @Override\n285:                 protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n286:                     return DefaultRecord.readPartiallyFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n287:                 }\n288:             };\n289:         } else {\n290:             return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n291:                 @Override\n292:                 protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n293:                     return DefaultRecord.readFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n294:                 }\n295:             };\n296:         }\n297:     }\n298: \n299:     private CloseableIterator<Record> uncompressedIterator() {\n300:         final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n301:         buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n302:         return new RecordIterator() {\n303:             @Override\n304:             protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n305:                 try {\n306:                     return DefaultRecord.readFrom(buffer, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n307:                 } catch (BufferUnderflowException e) {\n308:                     throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\");\n309:                 }\n310:             }\n311:             @Override\n312:             protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n313:                 return !buffer.hasRemaining();\n314:             }\n315:             @Override\n316:             public void close() {}\n317:         };\n318:     }\n319: \n320:     @Override\n321:     public Iterator<Record> iterator() {\n322:         if (count() == 0)\n323:             return Collections.emptyIterator();\n324: \n325:         if (!isCompressed())\n326:             return uncompressedIterator();\n327: \n328:         // for a normal iterator, we cannot ensure that the underlying compression stream is closed,\n329:         // so we decompress the full record set here. Use cases which call for a lower memory footprint\n330:         // can use `streamingIterator` at the cost of additional complexity\n331:         try (CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = compressedIterator(BufferSupplier.NO_CACHING, false)) {\n332:             List<Record> records = new ArrayList<>(count());\n333:             while (iterator.hasNext())\n334:                 records.add(iterator.next());\n335:             return records.iterator();\n336:         }\n337:     }\n338: \n339:     @Override\n340:     public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n341:         if (count() == 0) {\n342:             return CloseableIterator.wrap(Collections.emptyIterator());\n343:         }\n344: \n345:         /*\n346:          * For uncompressed iterator, it is actually not worth skipping key / value / headers at all since\n347:          * its ByteBufferInputStream's skip() function is less efficient compared with just reading it actually\n348:          * as it will allocate new byte array.\n349:          */\n350:         if (!isCompressed())\n351:             return uncompressedIterator();\n352: \n353:         // we define this to be a closable iterator so that caller (i.e. the log validator) needs to close it\n354:         // while we can save memory footprint of not decompressing the full record set ahead of time\n355:         return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, true);\n356:     }\n357: \n358:     @Override\n359:     public CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n360:         if (isCompressed())\n361:             return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, false);\n362:         else\n363:             return uncompressedIterator();\n364:     }\n365: \n366:     @Override\n367:     public void setLastOffset(long offset) {\n368:         buffer.putLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, offset - lastOffsetDelta());\n369:     }\n370: \n371:     @Override\n372:     public void setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType timestampType, long maxTimestamp) {\n373:         long currentMaxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n374:         // We don't need to recompute crc if the timestamp is not updated.\n375:         if (timestampType() == timestampType && currentMaxTimestamp == maxTimestamp)\n376:             return;\n377: \n378:         byte attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType(), timestampType, isTransactional(), isControlBatch(), hasDeleteHorizonMs());\n379:         buffer.putShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n380:         buffer.putLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n381:         long crc = computeChecksum();\n382:         ByteUtils.writeUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET, crc);\n383:     }\n384: \n385:     @Override\n386:     public void setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int epoch) {\n387:         buffer.putInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n388:     }\n389: \n390:     @Override\n391:     public long checksum() {\n392:         return ByteUtils.readUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET);\n393:     }\n394: \n395:     public boolean isValid() {\n396:         return sizeInBytes() >= RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD && checksum() == computeChecksum();\n397:     }\n398: \n399:     private long computeChecksum() {\n400:         return Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, buffer.limit() - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n401:     }\n402: \n403:     private byte attributes() {\n404:         // note we're not using the second byte of attributes\n405:         return (byte) buffer.getShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n406:     }\n407: \n408:     @Override\n409:     public boolean equals(Object o) {\n410:         if (this == o)\n411:             return true;\n412:         if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n413:             return false;\n414: \n415:         DefaultRecordBatch that = (DefaultRecordBatch) o;\n416:         return Objects.equals(buffer, that.buffer);\n417:     }\n418: \n419:     @Override\n420:     public int hashCode() {\n421:         return buffer != null ? buffer.hashCode() : 0;\n422:     }\n423: \n424:     private static byte computeAttributes(CompressionType type, TimestampType timestampType,\n425:                                           boolean isTransactional, boolean isControl, boolean isDeleteHorizonSet) {\n426:         if (timestampType == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE)\n427:             throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Timestamp type must be provided to compute attributes for message \" +\n428:                     \"format v2 and above\");\n429: \n430:         byte attributes = isTransactional ? TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK : 0;\n431:         if (isControl)\n432:             attributes |= CONTROL_FLAG_MASK;\n433:         if (type.id > 0)\n434:             attributes |= (byte) (COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK & type.id);\n435:         if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n436:             attributes |= TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK;\n437:         if (isDeleteHorizonSet)\n438:             attributes |= DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK;\n439:         return attributes;\n440:     }\n441: \n442:     public static void writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n443:                                         byte magic,\n444:                                         long producerId,\n445:                                         short producerEpoch,\n446:                                         int baseSequence,\n447:                                         long baseOffset,\n448:                                         long lastOffset,\n449:                                         int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n450:                                         TimestampType timestampType,\n451: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/DefaultRecordBatch.java%3FL1-450"
  }
}

ByteBufferOutputStream bufferStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(sizeEstimate);\\n671:         long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\\n672:         if (timestampType ==
