SimpleRecord... records) {\n625:         return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n626:                 baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n627:     }\n628: \n629:     public static MemoryRecords withIdempotentRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, long producerId,\n630:                                                       short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n631:                                                       SimpleRecord... records) {\n632:         return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n633:                 producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, false, records);\n634:     }\n635: \n636:     public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(Compression compression, long producerId,\n637:                                                          short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, SimpleRecord... records) {\n638:         return withRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, 0L, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME,\n639:                 producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, true, records);\n640:     }\n641: \n642:     public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n643:                                                          long producerId, short producerEpoch, int baseSequence,\n644:                                                          int partitionLeaderEpoch, SimpleRecord... records) {\n645:         return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, TimestampType.CREATE_TIME, producerId, producerEpoch,\n646:                 baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, true, records);\n647:     }\n648: \n649:     public static MemoryRecords withTransactionalRecords(long initialOffset, Compression compression, long producerId,\n650:                                                          short producerEpoch, int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n651:                                                          SimpleRecord... records) {\n652:         return withTransactionalRecords(RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, initialOffset, compression,\n653:                 producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, partitionLeaderEpoch, records);\n654:     }\n655: \n656:     public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n657:                                             TimestampType timestampType, SimpleRecord... records) {\n658:         return withRecords(magic, initialOffset, compression, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_ID,\n659:                 RecordBatch.NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH, RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n660:                 false, records);\n661:     }\n662: \n663:     public static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n664:                                             TimestampType timestampType, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n665:                                             int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch, boolean isTransactional,\n666:                                             SimpleRecord... records) {\n667:         if (records.length == 0)\n668:             return MemoryRecords.EMPTY;\n669:         int sizeEstimate = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytes(magic, compression.type(), Arrays.asList(records));\n670:         ByteBufferOutputStream bufferStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(sizeEstimate);\n671:         long logAppendTime = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n672:         if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n673:             logAppendTime = System.currentTimeMillis();\n674:         try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferStream, magic, compression, timestampType,\n675:                 initialOffset, logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, false,\n676:                 partitionLeaderEpoch, sizeEstimate)) {\n677:             for (SimpleRecord record : records)\n678:                 builder.append(record);\n679:             return builder.build();\n680:         }\n681:     }\n682: \n683:     public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long producerId, short producerEpoch, EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n684:         return withEndTransactionMarker(0L, System.currentTimeMillis(), RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH,\n685:                 producerId, producerEpoch, marker);\n686:     }\n687: \n688:     public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long timestamp, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n689:                                                          EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n690:         return withEndTransactionMarker(0L, timestamp, RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH, producerId,\n691:                 producerEpoch, marker);\n692:     }\n693: \n694:     public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long initialOffset, long timestamp, int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n695:                                                          long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n696:                                                          EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n697:         int endTxnMarkerBatchSize = DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + marker.endTxnMarkerValueSize();\n698:         ByteBuffer buffer = ByteBuffer.allocate(endTxnMarkerBatchSize);\n699:         writeEndTransactionalMarker(buffer, initialOffset, timestamp, partitionLeaderEpoch, producerId,\n700:                 producerEpoch, marker);\n701: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/kafka--e678b4b@47ac12bd6042edf23f1dbca3d789db945d62802d/-/blob/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/MemoryRecords.java%3FL550-700"
  }
}

14. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "new_string": "/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log format.\n *\n * <p>In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted of a single record\n * if no compression was enabled, but could contain many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic\n * versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records regardless of compression.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations of this interface are <b>not thread-safe</b>. External\n * synchronization is required for concurrent access.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are fundamental to the Kafka wire protocol and\n * the commit log storage format. They provide efficient serialization of multiple records with\n * shared metadata (producer ID, epoch, timestamps, compression). The batch format enables features\n * like idempotent producers, transactional support, and efficient log compaction.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, decompression is lazy\u2014records are\n * decompressed on-demand via iterators. For uncompressed batches, record data is directly accessible\n * from the backing buffer without additional copying.</p>\n *\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ implementation\n * @see MemoryRecords MemoryRecords for in-memory batch collections\n * @see Record Individual record in a batch\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {",
  "old_string": "/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
"Error: File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it."
15. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\n/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true If so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time, this is the max timestamp among all records contained in this batch and\n     * the value is updated during compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type",
    "filePath": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
    "numLines": 100,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 270
  },
  "type": "text"
}

  "file_path": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "new_string": "/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log format.\n *\n * <p>In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted of a single record\n * if no compression was enabled, but could contain many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic\n * versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records regardless of compression.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations of this interface are <b>not thread-safe</b>. External\n * synchronization is required for concurrent access.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are fundamental to the Kafka wire protocol and\n * the commit log storage format. They provide efficient serialization of multiple records with\n * shared metadata (producer ID, epoch, timestamps, compression). The batch format enables features\n * like idempotent producers, transactional support, and efficient log compaction.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, decompression is lazy\u2014records are\n * decompressed on-demand via iterators. For uncompressed batches, record data is directly accessible\n * from the backing buffer without additional copying.</p>\n *\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ implementation\n * @see MemoryRecords MemoryRecords for in-memory batch collections\n * @see Record Individual record in a batch\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {",
  "old_string": "/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "newString": "/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log format.\n *\n * <p>In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted of a single record\n * if no compression was enabled, but could contain many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic\n * versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records regardless of compression.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations of this interface are <b>not thread-safe</b>. External\n * synchronization is required for concurrent access.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are fundamental to the Kafka wire protocol and\n * the commit log storage format. They provide efficient serialization of multiple records with\n * shared metadata (producer ID, epoch, timestamps, compression). The batch format enables features\n * like idempotent producers, transactional support, and efficient log compaction.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, decompression is lazy\u2014records are\n * decompressed on-demand via iterators. For uncompressed batches, record data is directly accessible\n * from the backing buffer without additional copying.</p>\n *\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ implementation\n * @see MemoryRecords MemoryRecords for in-memory batch collections\n * @see Record Individual record in a batch\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {",
  "oldString": "/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\n/**\n * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),\n * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain\n * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records\n * regardless of compression.\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true If so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time, this is the max timestamp among all records contained in this batch and\n     * the value is updated during compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type\n     */\n    TimestampType timestampType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will\n     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will\n     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return\n     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the\n     * behavior is equivalent.\n     *\n     * Because this requires deep iteration for older magic versions, this method should be used with\n     * caution. Generally {@link #lastOffset()} is safer since access is efficient for all magic versions.\n     *\n     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record\n     *         as described above).\n     */\n    long baseOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset\n     * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The offset of the last record in this batch\n     */\n    long lastOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).\n     *\n     * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch\n     */\n    long nextOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).\n     *\n     * @return the magic byte\n     */\n    byte magic();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return -1.\n     *\n     * @return The producer id or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    long producerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The producer epoch, or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    short producerEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.\n     */\n    boolean hasProducerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #baseOffset()}, this value is not\n     * affected by compaction: it always retains the base sequence number from the original batch.\n     *\n     * @return The first sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int baseSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number\n     * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The last sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int lastSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the compression type of this record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The compression type\n     */\n    CompressionType compressionType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of the record and the batch overhead.\n     * @return The size in bytes of this batch\n     */\n    int sizeInBytes();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the count if it is efficiently supported by the record format (which is only the case\n     * for magic 2 and higher).\n     *\n     * @return The number of records in the batch or null for magic versions 0 and 1.\n     */\n    Integer countOrNull();\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.\n     * @return true if so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isCompressed();\n\n    /**\n     * Write this record batch into a buffer.\n     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to\n     */\n    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);\n\n    /**\n     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.\n     * @return true if it is, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isTransactional();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the delete horizon, returns OptionalLong.EMPTY if the first timestamp is not the delete horizon\n     * @return timestamp of the delete horizon\n     */\n    OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the partition leader epoch of this record batch.\n     * @return The leader epoch or -1 if it is unknown\n     */\n    int partitionLeaderEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records\n     * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.\n     *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return The closeable iterator\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).\n     * For magic versions prior to 2, this is always false.\n     *\n     * @return Whether this is a batch containing control records\n     */\n    boolean isControlBatch();\n\n    /**\n     * iterate all records to find the offset of max timestamp.\n     * noted:\n     * 1) that the earliest offset will return if there are multi records having same (max) timestamp\n     * 2) it always returns None if the {@link RecordBatch#magic()} is equal to {@link RecordBatch#MAGIC_VALUE_V0}\n     * @return offset of max timestamp\n     */\n    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n        if (magic() == RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V0) return Optional.empty();\n        long maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iter = streamingIterator(BufferSupplier.create())) {\n            while (iter.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iter.next();\n                if (maxTimestamp == record.timestamp()) return Optional.of(record.offset());\n            }\n        }\n        return Optional.empty();\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " import java.util.OptionalLong;",
        " ",
        " /**",
        "- * A record batch is a container for records. In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1),",
        "- * a batch consisted always of a single record if no compression was enabled, but could contain",
        "- * many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records",
        "- * regardless of compression.",
        "+ * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log format.",
        "+ *",
        "+ * <p>In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted of a single record",
        "+ * if no compression was enabled, but could contain many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic",
        "+ * versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records regardless of compression.</p>",
        "+ *",
        "+ * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations of this interface are <b>not thread-safe</b>. External",
        "+ * synchronization is required for concurrent access.</p>",
        "+ *",
        "+ * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are fundamental to the Kafka wire protocol and",
        "+ * the commit log storage format. They provide efficient serialization of multiple records with",
        "+ * shared metadata (producer ID, epoch, timestamps, compression). The batch format enables features",
        "+ * like idempotent producers, transactional support, and efficient log compaction.</p>",
        "+ *",
        "+ * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, decompression is lazy\u2014records are",
        "+ * decompressed on-demand via iterators. For uncompressed batches, record data is directly accessible",
        "+ * from the backing buffer without additional copying.</p>",
        "+ *",
        "+ * @see DefaultRecordBatch DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ implementation",
        "+ * @see MemoryRecords MemoryRecords for in-memory batch collections",
        "+ * @see Record Individual record in a batch",
        "  */",
        " public interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {",
        " "
      ],

17. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "new_string": "    /**\n     * Check whether the CRC-32C checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * <p>The checksum covers the batch header (from attributes byte onwards) and all record data.\n     * The partition leader epoch field is excluded from CRC computation to avoid recomputation\n     * when the field is assigned by the broker.</p>\n     *\n     * @return true if the computed checksum matches the stored checksum, false otherwise\n     * @see #checksum() to retrieve the stored checksum value\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum of this batch is not valid.\n     *\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the stored checksum does not match the computed checksum\n     * @see #isValid() for non-exceptional validation\n     */\n    void ensureValid();",
  "old_string": "    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true If so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     */\n    void ensureValid();",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Check whether the CRC-32C checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * <p>The checksum covers the batch header (from attributes byte onwards) and all record data.\n     * The partition leader epoch field is excluded from CRC computation to avoid recomputation\n     * when the field is assigned by the broker.</p>\n     *\n     * @return true if the computed checksum matches the stored checksum, false otherwise\n     * @see #checksum() to retrieve the stored checksum value\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum of this batch is not valid.\n     *\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the stored checksum does not match the computed checksum\n     * @see #isValid() for non-exceptional validation\n     */\n    void ensureValid();",
  "oldString": "    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true If so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     */\n    void ensureValid();",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\n/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log format.\n *\n * <p>In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted of a single record\n * if no compression was enabled, but could contain many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic\n * versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records regardless of compression.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations of this interface are <b>not thread-safe</b>. External\n * synchronization is required for concurrent access.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are fundamental to the Kafka wire protocol and\n * the commit log storage format. They provide efficient serialization of multiple records with\n * shared metadata (producer ID, epoch, timestamps, compression). The batch format enables features\n * like idempotent producers, transactional support, and efficient log compaction.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, decompression is lazy\u2014records are\n * decompressed on-demand via iterators. For uncompressed batches, record data is directly accessible\n * from the backing buffer without additional copying.</p>\n *\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ implementation\n * @see MemoryRecords MemoryRecords for in-memory batch collections\n * @see Record Individual record in a batch\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * @return true If so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time, this is the max timestamp among all records contained in this batch and\n     * the value is updated during compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type\n     */\n    TimestampType timestampType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will\n     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will\n     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return\n     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the\n     * behavior is equivalent.\n     *\n     * Because this requires deep iteration for older magic versions, this method should be used with\n     * caution. Generally {@link #lastOffset()} is safer since access is efficient for all magic versions.\n     *\n     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record\n     *         as described above).\n     */\n    long baseOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset\n     * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The offset of the last record in this batch\n     */\n    long lastOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).\n     *\n     * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch\n     */\n    long nextOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).\n     *\n     * @return the magic byte\n     */\n    byte magic();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return -1.\n     *\n     * @return The producer id or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    long producerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The producer epoch, or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    short producerEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.\n     */\n    boolean hasProducerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #baseOffset()}, this value is not\n     * affected by compaction: it always retains the base sequence number from the original batch.\n     *\n     * @return The first sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int baseSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number\n     * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The last sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int lastSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the compression type of this record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The compression type\n     */\n    CompressionType compressionType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of the record and the batch overhead.\n     * @return The size in bytes of this batch\n     */\n    int sizeInBytes();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the count if it is efficiently supported by the record format (which is only the case\n     * for magic 2 and higher).\n     *\n     * @return The number of records in the batch or null for magic versions 0 and 1.\n     */\n    Integer countOrNull();\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.\n     * @return true if so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isCompressed();\n\n    /**\n     * Write this record batch into a buffer.\n     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to\n     */\n    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);\n\n    /**\n     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.\n     * @return true if it is, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isTransactional();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the delete horizon, returns OptionalLong.EMPTY if the first timestamp is not the delete horizon\n     * @return timestamp of the delete horizon\n     */\n    OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the partition leader epoch of this record batch.\n     * @return The leader epoch or -1 if it is unknown\n     */\n    int partitionLeaderEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records\n     * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.\n     *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return The closeable iterator\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).\n     * For magic versions prior to 2, this is always false.\n     *\n     * @return Whether this is a batch containing control records\n     */\n    boolean isControlBatch();\n\n    /**\n     * iterate all records to find the offset of max timestamp.\n     * noted:\n     * 1) that the earliest offset will return if there are multi records having same (max) timestamp\n     * 2) it always returns None if the {@link RecordBatch#magic()} is equal to {@link RecordBatch#MAGIC_VALUE_V0}\n     * @return offset of max timestamp\n     */\n    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n        if (magic() == RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V0) return Optional.empty();\n        long maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iter = streamingIterator(BufferSupplier.create())) {\n            while (iter.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iter.next();\n                if (maxTimestamp == record.timestamp()) return Optional.of(record.offset());\n            }\n        }\n        return Optional.empty();\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;",
        " ",
        "     /**",
        "-     * Check whether the checksum of this batch is correct.",
        "+     * Check whether the CRC-32C checksum of this batch is correct.",
        "      *",
        "-     * @return true If so, false otherwise",
        "+     * <p>The checksum covers the batch header (from attributes byte onwards) and all record data.",
        "+     * The partition leader epoch field is excluded from CRC computation to avoid recomputation",
        "+     * when the field is assigned by the broker.</p>",
        "+     *",
        "+     * @return true if the computed checksum matches the stored checksum, false otherwise",
        "+     * @see #checksum() to retrieve the stored checksum value",
        "      */",
        "     boolean isValid();",
        " ",
        "     /**",
        "-     * Raise an exception if the checksum is not valid.",
        "+     * Raise an exception if the checksum of this batch is not valid.",
        "+     *",
        "+     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the stored checksum does not match the computed checksum",
        "+     * @see #isValid() for non-exceptional validation",

18. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "new_string": "    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch.\n     *\n     * <p>For magic version prior to 2, this returns the offset of the first record in the batch,\n     * which generally requires deep iteration. For magic version 2 and above, this returns the\n     * first offset of the original record batch (prior to compaction), which is stored directly in\n     * the batch header and is efficiently accessible. For non-compacted topics, the behavior is\n     * equivalent across versions.</p>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance Note:</b> For older magic versions (v0, v1), this method requires iterating\n     * through records and should be used with caution. {@link #lastOffset()} is safer and more\n     * efficient for all magic versions.</p>\n     *\n     * @return the base offset of this record batch\n     * @see #lastOffset() for the more efficiently accessible last offset\n     */\n    long baseOffset();",
  "old_string": "    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will\n     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will\n     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return\n     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the\n     * behavior is equivalent.\n     *\n     * Because this requires deep iteration for older magic versions, this method should be used with\n     * caution. Generally {@link #lastOffset()} is safer since access is efficient for all magic versions.\n     *\n     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record\n     *         as described above).\n     */\n    long baseOffset();",
  "replace_all": false
}

{

Newer versions (magic\n * versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records regardless of compression.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations of this interface are <b>not thread-safe</b>. External\n * synchronization is required for concurrent access.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are fundamental to the Kafka wire protocol and\n * the commit log storage format. They provide efficient serialization of multiple records with\n * shared metadata (producer ID, epoch, timestamps, compression). The batch format enables features\n * like idempotent producers, transactional support, and efficient log compaction.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, decompression is lazy\u2014records are\n * decompressed on-demand via iterators. For uncompressed batches, record data is directly accessible\n * from the backing buffer without additional copying.</p>\n *\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ implementation\n * @see MemoryRecords MemoryRecords for in-memory batch collections\n * @see Record Individual record in a batch\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the CRC-32C checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * <p>The checksum covers the batch header (from attributes byte onwards) and all record data.\n     * The partition leader epoch field is excluded from CRC computation to avoid recomputation\n     * when the field is assigned by the broker.</p>\n     *\n     * @return true if the computed checksum matches the stored checksum, false otherwise\n     * @see #checksum() to retrieve the stored checksum value\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum of this batch is not valid.\n     *\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the stored checksum does not match the computed checksum\n     * @see #isValid() for non-exceptional validation\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time, this is the max timestamp among all records contained in this batch and\n     * the value is updated during compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type\n     */\n    TimestampType timestampType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will\n     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will\n     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return\n     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the\n     * behavior is equivalent.\n     *\n     * Because this requires deep iteration for older magic versions, this method should be used with\n     * caution. Generally {@link #lastOffset()} is safer since access is efficient for all magic versions.\n     *\n     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record\n     *         as described above).\n     */\n    long baseOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset\n     * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The offset of the last record in this batch\n     */\n    long lastOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).\n     *\n     * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch\n     */\n    long nextOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).\n     *\n     * @return the magic byte\n     */\n    byte magic();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return -1.\n     *\n     * @return The producer id or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    long producerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The producer epoch, or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    short producerEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.\n     */\n    boolean hasProducerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #baseOffset()}, this value is not\n     * affected by compaction: it always retains the base sequence number from the original batch.\n     *\n     * @return The first sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int baseSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number\n     * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The last sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int lastSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the compression type of this record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The compression type\n     */\n    CompressionType compressionType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of the record and the batch overhead.\n     * @return The size in bytes of this batch\n     */\n    int sizeInBytes();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the count if it is efficiently supported by the record format (which is only the case\n     * for magic 2 and higher).\n     *\n     * @return The number of records in the batch or null for magic versions 0 and 1.\n     */\n    Integer countOrNull();\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.\n     * @return true if so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isCompressed();\n\n    /**\n     * Write this record batch into a buffer.\n     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to\n     */\n    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);\n\n    /**\n     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.\n     * @return true if it is, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isTransactional();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the delete horizon, returns OptionalLong.EMPTY if the first timestamp is not the delete horizon\n     * @return timestamp of the delete horizon\n     */\n    OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the partition leader epoch of this record batch.\n     * @return The leader epoch or -1 if it is unknown\n     */\n    int partitionLeaderEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records\n     * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.\n     *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return The closeable iterator\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).\n     * For magic versions prior to 2, this is always false.\n     *\n     * @return Whether this is a batch containing control records\n     */\n    boolean isControlBatch();\n\n    /**\n     * iterate all records to find the offset of max timestamp.\n     * noted:\n     * 1) that the earliest offset will return if there are multi records having same (max) timestamp\n     * 2) it always returns None if the {@link RecordBatch#magic()} is equal to {@link RecordBatch#MAGIC_VALUE_V0}\n     * @return offset of max timestamp\n     */\n    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n        if (magic() == RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V0) return Optional.empty();\n        long maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iter = streamingIterator(BufferSupplier.create())) {\n            while (iter.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iter.next();\n                if (maxTimestamp == record.timestamp()) return Optional.of(record.offset());\n            }\n        }\n        return Optional.empty();\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     TimestampType timestampType();",
        " ",
        "     /**",
        "-     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch. For magic version prior to 2, the base offset will",
        "-     * always be the offset of the first message in the batch. This generally requires deep iteration and will",
        "-     * return the offset of the first record in the record batch. For magic version 2 and above, this will return",
        "-     * the first offset of the original record batch (i.e. prior to compaction). For non-compacted topics, the",
        "-     * behavior is equivalent.",
        "+     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch.",
        "      *",
        "-     * Because this requires deep iteration for older magic versions, this method should be used with",
        "-     * caution. Generally {@link #lastOffset()} is safer since access is efficient for all magic versions.",
        "+     * <p>For magic version prior to 2, this returns the offset of the first record in the batch,",
        "+     * which generally requires deep iteration. For magic version 2 and above, this returns the",
        "+     * first offset of the original record batch (prior to compaction), which is stored directly in",
        "+     * the batch header and is efficiently accessible. For non-compacted topics, the behavior is",
        "+     * equivalent across versions.</p>",
        "      *",
        "-     * @return The base offset of this record batch (which may or may not be the offset of the first record",
        "-     *         as described above).",
        "+     * <p><b>Performance Note:</b> For older magic versions (v0, v1), this method requires iterating",
        "+     * through records and should be used with caution. {@link #lastOffset()} is safer and more",
        "+     * efficient for all magic versions.</p>",
        "+     *",
        "+     * @return the base offset of this record batch",
        "+     * @see #lastOffset() for the more efficiently accessible last offset",
        "      */",
        "     long baseOffset();",
        " "
      ],
      "newLines": 20,
      "newStart": 127,
      "oldLines": 17,
      "oldStart": 127
    }

19. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "new_string": "    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator that delays decompression of the record stream until records are\n     * actually requested via {@link Iterator#next()}.\n     *\n     * <p>If the message format does not support streaming iteration (e.g., legacy formats), the\n     * normal iterator is returned instead. This method enables memory-efficient processing of\n     * compressed batches by decompressing records on-demand rather than upfront.</p>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, this avoids allocating large\n     * decompression buffers upfront. For small record batches, the cost of allocating a buffer\n     * (e.g., 64 KB for LZ4) can dominate decompression cost. Using a {@code decompressionBufferSupplier}\n     * that reuses buffers across multiple batches can significantly reduce allocation overhead.</p>\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier supplier of ByteBuffer(s) for decompression. Using a\n     *        reusable buffer supplier has significant performance impact for small batches.\n     *        {@link BufferSupplier#NO_CACHING} creates a new buffer per decompression.\n     *\n     * @return a closeable iterator over records in this batch. The caller must close this iterator\n     *         to release decompression resources.\n     *\n     * @see BufferSupplier for buffer management strategies\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);",
  "old_string": "    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records\n     * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.\n     *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return The closeable iterator\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/kafka-repo/clients/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/common/record/internal/RecordBatch.java",
  "newString": "    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator that delays decompression of the record stream until records are\n     * actually requested via {@link Iterator#next()}.\n     *\n     * <p>If the message format does not support streaming iteration (e.g., legacy formats), the\n     * normal iterator is returned instead. This method enables memory-efficient processing of\n     * compressed batches by decompressing records on-demand rather than upfront.</p>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, this avoids allocating large\n     * decompression buffers upfront. For small record batches, the cost of allocating a buffer\n     * (e.g., 64 KB for LZ4) can dominate decompression cost. Using a {@code decompressionBufferSupplier}\n     * that reuses buffers across multiple batches can significantly reduce allocation overhead.</p>\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier supplier of ByteBuffer(s) for decompression. Using a\n     *        reusable buffer supplier has significant performance impact for small batches.\n     *        {@link BufferSupplier#NO_CACHING} creates a new buffer per decompression.\n     *\n     * @return a closeable iterator over records in this batch. The caller must close this iterator\n     *         to release decompression resources.\n     *\n     * @see BufferSupplier for buffer management strategies\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);",
  "oldString": "    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records\n     * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.\n     *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return The closeable iterator\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);",
  "originalFile": "/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at\n *\n *    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n *\n * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n * distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n * limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.record.TimestampType;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.BufferSupplier;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\n\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.Optional;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\n/**\n * A record batch is a container for records in the Kafka log format.\n *\n * <p>In old versions of the record format (versions 0 and 1), a batch consisted of a single record\n * if no compression was enabled, but could contain many records otherwise. Newer versions (magic\n * versions 2 and above) will generally contain many records regardless of compression.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Thread Safety:</b> Implementations of this interface are <b>not thread-safe</b>. External\n * synchronization is required for concurrent access.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Role in Kafka Protocol:</b> Record batches are fundamental to the Kafka wire protocol and\n * the commit log storage format. They provide efficient serialization of multiple records with\n * shared metadata (producer ID, epoch, timestamps, compression). The batch format enables features\n * like idempotent producers, transactional support, and efficient log compaction.</p>\n *\n * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, decompression is lazy\u2014records are\n * decompressed on-demand via iterators. For uncompressed batches, record data is directly accessible\n * from the backing buffer without additional copying.</p>\n *\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch DefaultRecordBatch for magic v2+ implementation\n * @see MemoryRecords MemoryRecords for in-memory batch collections\n * @see Record Individual record in a batch\n */\npublic interface RecordBatch extends Iterable<Record> {\n\n    /**\n     * The \"magic\" values\n     */\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V0 = 0;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V1 = 1;\n    byte MAGIC_VALUE_V2 = 2;\n\n    /**\n     * The current \"magic\" value\n     */\n    byte CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE = MAGIC_VALUE_V2;\n\n    /**\n     * Timestamp value for records without a timestamp\n     */\n    long NO_TIMESTAMP = -1L;\n\n    /**\n     * Values used in the v2 record format by non-idempotent/non-transactional producers or when\n     * up-converting from an older format.\n     */\n    long NO_PRODUCER_ID = -1L;\n    short NO_PRODUCER_EPOCH = -1;\n    int NO_SEQUENCE = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Used to indicate an unknown leader epoch, which will be the case when the record set is\n     * first created by the producer.\n     */\n    int NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH = -1;\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether the CRC-32C checksum of this batch is correct.\n     *\n     * <p>The checksum covers the batch header (from attributes byte onwards) and all record data.\n     * The partition leader epoch field is excluded from CRC computation to avoid recomputation\n     * when the field is assigned by the broker.</p>\n     *\n     * @return true if the computed checksum matches the stored checksum, false otherwise\n     * @see #checksum() to retrieve the stored checksum value\n     */\n    boolean isValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Raise an exception if the checksum of this batch is not valid.\n     *\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if the stored checksum does not match the computed checksum\n     * @see #isValid() for non-exceptional validation\n     */\n    void ensureValid();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the checksum of this record batch, which covers the batch header as well as all of the records.\n     *\n     * @return The 4-byte unsigned checksum represented as a long\n     */\n    long checksum();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the max timestamp or log append time of this record batch.\n     *\n     * If the timestamp type is create time, this is the max timestamp among all records contained in this batch and\n     * the value is updated during compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The max timestamp\n     */\n    long maxTimestamp();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the timestamp type of this record batch. This will be {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE}\n     * if the batch has magic 0.\n     *\n     * @return The timestamp type\n     */\n    TimestampType timestampType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base offset contained in this record batch.\n     *\n     * <p>For magic version prior to 2, this returns the offset of the first record in the batch,\n     * which generally requires deep iteration. For magic version 2 and above, this returns the\n     * first offset of the original record batch (prior to compaction), which is stored directly in\n     * the batch header and is efficiently accessible. For non-compacted topics, the behavior is\n     * equivalent across versions.</p>\n     *\n     * <p><b>Performance Note:</b> For older magic versions (v0, v1), this method requires iterating\n     * through records and should be used with caution. {@link #lastOffset()} is safer and more\n     * efficient for all magic versions.</p>\n     *\n     * @return the base offset of this record batch\n     * @see #lastOffset() for the more efficiently accessible last offset\n     */\n    long baseOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last offset in this record batch (inclusive). Just like {@link #baseOffset()}, the last offset\n     * always reflects the offset of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The offset of the last record in this batch\n     */\n    long lastOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the offset following this record batch (i.e. the last offset contained in this batch plus one).\n     *\n     * @return the next consecutive offset following this batch\n     */\n    long nextOffset();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the record format version of this record batch (i.e its magic value).\n     *\n     * @return the magic byte\n     */\n    byte magic();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer id for this log record batch. For older magic versions, this will return -1.\n     *\n     * @return The producer id or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    long producerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the producer epoch for this log record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The producer epoch, or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    short producerEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Does the batch have a valid producer id set.\n     */\n    boolean hasProducerId();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #baseOffset()}, this value is not\n     * affected by compaction: it always retains the base sequence number from the original batch.\n     *\n     * @return The first sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int baseSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the last sequence number of this record batch. Like {@link #lastOffset()}, the last sequence number\n     * always reflects the sequence number of the last record in the original batch, even if it is removed during log\n     * compaction.\n     *\n     * @return The last sequence number or -1 if there is none\n     */\n    int lastSequence();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the compression type of this record batch.\n     *\n     * @return The compression type\n     */\n    CompressionType compressionType();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the size in bytes of this batch, including the size of the record and the batch overhead.\n     * @return The size in bytes of this batch\n     */\n    int sizeInBytes();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the count if it is efficiently supported by the record format (which is only the case\n     * for magic 2 and higher).\n     *\n     * @return The number of records in the batch or null for magic versions 0 and 1.\n     */\n    Integer countOrNull();\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this record batch is compressed.\n     * @return true if so, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isCompressed();\n\n    /**\n     * Write this record batch into a buffer.\n     * @param buffer The buffer to write the batch to\n     */\n    void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer);\n\n    /**\n     * Whether or not this record batch is part of a transaction.\n     * @return true if it is, false otherwise\n     */\n    boolean isTransactional();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the delete horizon, returns OptionalLong.EMPTY if the first timestamp is not the delete horizon\n     * @return timestamp of the delete horizon\n     */\n    OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs();\n\n    /**\n     * Get the partition leader epoch of this record batch.\n     * @return The leader epoch or -1 if it is unknown\n     */\n    int partitionLeaderEpoch();\n\n    /**\n     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records\n     * are actually asked for using {@link Iterator#next()}. If the message format does not support streaming\n     * iteration, then the normal iterator is returned. Either way, callers should ensure that the iterator is closed.\n     *\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported.\n     *                                    For small record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4)\n     *                                    will dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return The closeable iterator\n     */\n    CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier);\n\n    /**\n     * Check whether this is a control batch (i.e. whether the control bit is set in the batch attributes).\n     * For magic versions prior to 2, this is always false.\n     *\n     * @return Whether this is a batch containing control records\n     */\n    boolean isControlBatch();\n\n    /**\n     * iterate all records to find the offset of max timestamp.\n     * noted:\n     * 1) that the earliest offset will return if there are multi records having same (max) timestamp\n     * 2) it always returns None if the {@link RecordBatch#magic()} is equal to {@link RecordBatch#MAGIC_VALUE_V0}\n     * @return offset of max timestamp\n     */\n    default Optional<Long> offsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n        if (magic() == RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V0) return Optional.empty();\n        long maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iter = streamingIterator(BufferSupplier.create())) {\n            while (iter.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iter.next();\n                if (maxTimestamp == record.timestamp()) return Optional.of(record.offset());\n            }\n        }\n        return Optional.empty();\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,

    {

        "     /**",
        "-     * Return a streaming iterator which basically delays decompression of the record stream until the records",

        "+     *",
        "+     * <p><b>Performance Characteristics:</b> For compressed batches, this avoids allocating large",
        "+     * decompression buffers upfront. For small record batches, the cost of allocating a buffer",
        "+     * (e.g., 64 KB for LZ4) can dominate decompression cost. Using a {@code decompressionBufferSupplier}",
        "+     * that reuses buffers across multiple batches can significantly reduce allocation overhead.</p>",
